prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for neutral formaldehyde (CH₂O) using DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional and basis (tier standard), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, via PySCF. The geometry was a single-point calculation on the supplied coordinates (4 atoms). Three sets of Fukui indices were calculated by computing Mulliken atomic charges on the neutral molecule, its +1 cation, and its −1 anion: f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index) indicates sensitivity to nucleophilic attack; f⁻ (nucleophilic Fukui index) indicates sensitivity to electrophilic attack; f⁰ is their average (radical Fukui index). The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) predicts whether a site favors electrophilic or nucleophilic activation. SCF converged; Fukui charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0, sum(f⁻) = 1.0). Most electrophilic site: carbon (f⁺ = 0.7084). Most nucleophilic site: oxygen (f⁻ = 0.4674). Integrity status: trustworthy = true.
